rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN INTER VS COCA-COLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: GRANT Coca-Cola's Motion for Summary Adjudication re duty. There is no triable issue of fact re coverage for 'workplace torts' or whether Evans asserted claims for misrepresentation under 17200 and coercion under Labor?206.5 [improperly obtaining release. American has a duty to indemnify as follows: (1) American has a duty to indemnify for the portion of the settlement that represents attorney fees; (2) American has a duty to indemnify for the portion of the settlement that represents cost of class administration; (3) American has a duty to indemnify for the portion of the settlement which represents compensatory damages, i.e. 50% of each claim. DENY American's 437c(h) request because affidavit filed in support fails to show why deposition is necessary, why Coca-Cola's employees could not have made declarations, and facts showing Taylor is likely to offer deposition testimony contrary to her declaration. DENY Coca-Cola's MSA regarding breach. This is not an appropriate issue for adjudication under 437c(f)(1). (CW)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN INTER VS COCA-COLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: GRANT American's Motion for Summary Adjudication as follows: American does not have a duty to indemnify for the portion of the settlement which represents earned but unpaid wages, i.e. %50 of each claim. (CW)

rulings.Case Title: WRIGHT VS GEMPLUS S.A.
